raw_response: I concur that Option B (citrate) is correct. Citrate is indeed chosen because it minimally affects ion concentrations compared to alternatives and helps prevent hemolysis which could otherwise distort electrolyte readings. This aligns perfectly with optimizing accuracy in electrolyte assessments where minimal alteration of sample integrity is crucial. Hence, my agreement stands firm on selecting B as the best anticoagulant for electrolyte estimation. Answer Choice: B.
